description: Overlay for argumentative position papers (e.g. the canary's 'latency wall' thesis). A position paper names a problem and argues a viewpoint; it does NOT recommend an investment or carry a check size. Financial reasoning is typically out of scope — the paper engages technical / strategic / market shape and leaves capital allocation to the reader. Risk honesty is preserved mostly intact: theoretical / strategic risks to the argument matter even when venture-specific risks (run-rate burn, dilution, deal terms) do not. Per #283 sub-deliverable 3 seed analysis (originally from closed #278).

calibration_prose.dim_1: position-paper — score on whether the position itself is sharply stated and defended, not on whether the paper names a single investment recommendation with check size. A position paper that clearly says 'X will happen because Y' at full force scores at full (reduced) weight; vague hedging on the position scores down.
calibration_prose.dim_4: position-paper — score on whether risks to the argument (counter-evidence, alternative interpretations, scenarios that would falsify the position) are honestly named, not on whether venture-specific risks (burn rate, deal terms, founder concentration) are enumerated. Theoretical / strategic risk discipline is the target.
calibration_prose.dim_6: position-paper — financial reasoning is largely out of scope. Score on whether financial framing supports the position when invoked (e.g. unit economics cited to anchor a 'why now' argument), not on whether the paper does original unit-economics or scenario modeling. Most position papers will score at the top of the reduced weight without doing any financial work.
